title: Room 1: The Locked Study
description: A wall safe hums behind a framed quote. Dusty books hide three symbols etched into the desk.
question: What keyword in programming usually means 'if this is true, do the next thing'?
hint: Think about code deciding between paths.
success: The safe clicks open and a brass key slides out.

title: Room 2: The Server Hall
description: Rows of monitors flicker to life. One terminal demands that you identify the user-facing layer.
question: Which option best describes the front end of a web app?
answers: The part users see and interact with
hint: It is the layer that shapes the user's experience.
success: The monitors turn green and a hidden door unlocks.

title: Room 3: The Archive Vault
description: Steel drawers line the walls. A note says the right answer keeps information organized and reusable.
question: What structure is commonly used to store related values under named keys?
success: A drawer slides open and reveals the next access card.

title: Room 4: The Signal Chamber
description: Copper wires connect old panels to a sealed hatch. A blinking prompt asks what lets nodes react to events.
question: In Godot, what feature is used to react when one node notifies another about an event?
hint: Buttons often use this to tell scripts they were pressed.
success: The panel crackles, and the hatch unlocks with a low thud.

title: Room 5: The Reflection Chamber
description: Mirrors reflect your progress back at you. The final door asks for the mindset that keeps hard projects moving.
question: What helps most when building something challenging step by step?
success: The final lock opens. You escaped.
